answer:
**Viṣṇu Smṛti (Vishnu Smriti), Chapter 1** opens with this exact narrative in its introductory verses.[^1]

Vishnu incarnates as a cosmic Boar to rescue the Earth from the waters of dissolution: "Took on the auspicious form of the Boar... And lifted up the Earth."[^2] The Boar's form symbolically embodies the Vedic sacrifice, "whose tusks were the sacrificial post... Whose tongue was the sacrificial fire."[^3]

After recreating the world and vanishing to "a destination then unknown to the worlds,"[^4] the Earth (Vasudhā or Dhara) ponders her support: "What will be my support now?" She resolves to consult the sage Kashyapa.[^5]

Taking "the form of a woman," with "lips auspicious and red like the Bandhujīva flower," she approaches him.[^6] Kashyapa directs her to Vishnu in the "Ocean of Milk," where she finds him "resting upon the couch of Śeṣa" (the reclining god).[^7]

There, she asks again: "Now, therefore, O Lord of gods, what will be my support?" Vishnu replies that good people devoted to *varṇas* and *āśramas* will support her, then seats her "on this excellent golden throne" to expound the law (*dharma*): "Be seated, O you of beautiful thighs, on this excellent golden throne. Seated comfortably, hear now the laws as I declare them to you."[^8]

[^1]: *Viṣṇu Smṛti*, [Chapter 1](/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/01/translation/).

[^2]: *Viṣṇu Smṛti* 1.2, [p-bb3692](/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/01/translation/#p-bb3692).

[^3]: *Viṣṇu Smṛti* 1.3–5, [p-979e7a](/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/01/translation/#p-979e7a).

[^4]: *Viṣṇu Smṛti* 1.18, [p-d6fb26](/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/01/translation/#p-d6fb26).

[^5]: *Viṣṇu Smṛti* 1.19–20, [p-7b2100](/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/01/translation/#p-7b2100).

[^6]: *Viṣṇu Smṛti* 1.21–22, [p-7965d8](/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/01/translation/#p-7965d8).

[^7]: *Viṣṇu Smṛti* 1.31–33, 40, [p-2b042c](/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/01/translation/#p-2b042c).

[^8]: *Viṣṇu Smṛti* 1.46, 64, [p-5eec19](/works/unknown-visnu-smrti-27f0d8/01/translation/#p-5eec19).
